title: appertiella hexandra
intro: Appertiella hexandra, also known as the Indian alkanet or Indian hollyhock, is a plant species native to India and parts of Southeast Asia. The plant's roots and leaves have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Appertiella hexandra include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, decoctions, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the roots and leaves in hot water to release their medicinal properties.
